title: ChefSofi 1.5 Cup-Capacity Mortar and Pestle Set - Unpolished Heavy Granite for Enhanced Performance and Organic Appearance
description:
About this item
A kitchen must-have: Mortar and pestle set (mortero de cocina - morter and pessel) has been used for THOUSANDS of years as THE way to crush, grind and powder herbs and dry spices. Contrary to an electric grinder or crusher, the age-old, durable, traditional manual grinding method ensures that all cooking ingredients bring out their full flavor and aroma profiles, allowing you to further control their texture and make delicious, chunk-free dishes.
Versatile tool: Take advantage of your brand new stone motar and pedestal set’s various applications in the kitchen and simplify your everyday life! Use your mortor to pulverize nuts, seeds, ginger root and garlic and make homemade salad dressing, sauces and condiments, such as fresh mustard, quacamole, pesto, salsa, chutneys and more. Widely used in pharmacies and apothecaries, your molcajete set will help you powder pills, for optimal ingestion, or hide them in your stubborn pet’s kibble!
Effortless use: This ChefSofi stone mocajetes motor & pedestal set was designed with your convenience in mind. Made from unpolished granite, you will waste no time fumbling or stabilizing your pestal masher, as our motar’s cup interior provides the best, metate-like natural friction for swift ingredient crushing, grinding or powdering.
Quick cleaning & zero kitchen messes: As granite is inherently durable and heavy, this mortar will not slip on your kitchen counters, remaining still throughout the grinding process. Our pestle offers foolproof grip, gliding across the mortar’s interior, without the need of excess hand motions, which would result in ingredient waste and kitchen counter messes! By investing in this stoneware set, you are gaining a timeless, multifunctional kitchen tool, which will make cooking a breeze!
Satisfaction guarantee: We want you to be absolutely elated with your moter and pestle set, which is why we are offering you a Risk-Free purchase. In the unlikely event our set fails to meet or exceed your expectations, you are welcome to return it hassle-free. Purchase Now The Best, Unpolished Marble - Granite Mortar & Pestle Set On The Market!
reviews_5s:
Play Video
The ChefSofi Mortar and Pestle Set is an absolute gem in the kitchen! It’s a heavy-duty, sturdy set that feels great in your hands, making it easy for all your grinding needs. It comes with detailed instructions on how to prepare it before first use, which is super helpful.

We primarily use it to make ginger-garlic paste, which works like a charm and enhances the flavor of our cooking. The 1.5-cup capacity is just right for our needs. A thoughtful addition is the mat that sticks to the bottom, keeping it stable during use. Cleanup is a breeze, which makes it even better.

If you’re looking for a reliable and well-designed mortar and pestle, this one is a winner!Play Video
I had a smaller mortar and pestle that I used to like, but since we grow and grind our own herbs and spices, I needed larger one. This mortar and pestle was the perfect size that I needed. It’s not a lightweight either just the motor and pestle weighs in at 7 1/2 pounds if I removed the mortar it’s a little over 6 pounds so it tells you how heavy duty this marble is. The outside is very smooth. It’s black marble very heavy duty very sturdy. I’m very happy with it you’re in need of mortar and pestle I would highly recommend this brand. I’m able to mix seasonings of any kind in there and then price was very reasonable for the heavy duty of this mortar and pestle It’s very easy to hold. It’s very versatile. To do There is no lid and I don’t create any dust.A sturdy m&p that comes with amazing instructions for care and seasoning. Includes a very necessary thin felt mat for the bottom of the mortar so as it doesn’t damage countertops or slide away. Very attractive so I have chosen to leave it out on display. Very comfortable to grip and use and easy to clean.
Would buy again but I have a feeling this will last a lifetime!I’ve used it way more times than I was expecting. Very heavy and sturdy, works well with grinding and mashing things.

I like that it comes with a thin foam pad that you stick on the bottom to minimize impact with your counter. I still recommend using it over a small towel or something to dampen the impact on your counter and the sound that makes.

It is heavy so it can be hard to pick up, empty it’s contents, clean etc. So that’s something to consider for those who might have hand/arm pain or weakness.

It also is sizeable so need to find a place in my kitchen for yet another gadget but I do think it is worth the space. .

Comes with very clear, simple and concise instructions for first time use and overall care (never use soap!). If they hadn’t had these instructions written very visibly and clearly I probably would have done it all wrong. Thankfully it caught my attention and all is well.

I like that this is kind of like a wok. It soaks in a bit of whatever you are grinding and over time it can add seasoning back into what you grind in it.

I now use it regularly to grind up my spices for chai tea, mash up ginger or garlic for recipes. Crush up peppers. Made ground roasted sesame for a homemade Chinese sesame paste. Very happy with this purchase.Heavy duty and I love the seasoning instructions that came with it!This ChefSofi Mortar and Pestle Set - 6 Inch - 2 Cup Capacity - Unpolished Heavy Granite for Enhanced Performance and Organic Appearance - Included: Anti-Scratch Protector is really nice and came with great instructions. I even got an email from the seller with more information and recipes.

I don't know why I waited so long to get one of these. What prompted me was this last hurricane (Milton) where I was out of power for several days and still wanted to grind my daily whole flaxseeds. I was putting them in a silicone baggie and pounding them with a sledge hammer. That didn't work out so well. I decided to get a mortar and pestle and this one seemed the nicest. This works a lot better than the sledgehammer for grinding my flaxseeds.Very durable and heavy. Works perfectly! I love it!In love. When I first got the box I was initially really disappointed because I was like what can I even put in it it looks small and then you take it out it is pretty deep and the perfect size. And looks amazing and fits in perfectly on my alterDoes great job with nearly anything that can be crushed. It DOES however require some breaking in to smooth out the inside and remove any stray bit of stone left over from manufacture. The instructions make his point and are very clear. Great product should last forever!I originally was rating this as 3 stars as I did not receive the not scratch bottom. I wrote to the seller and in less than 24 hours offered to send one to me. I could not ask for any better support and the product is what I wanted.
reviews_1s:
I was disappointed to see that the "clean and season" instructions that come with this instruct one to repeatedly grind rice until stone dust (carcinogenic by the way) stops coming off the mortar. Or in other words, we didn't bother polishing the grinding surface, so you have to do it before you can actually use this thing for food.I have used mortars and pestles in the kitchen since I was five years old mashing up spices for mom's chicken pot pie. My grind stroke has been perfected through thousands of meals, and not only have I never broken one during use, but I've never even met someone who has. I usually end up getting a new one every 5 or so years for whatever reason, dropping them or getting lost in a move or even once a theft.

When I first received this mortar and pestle, I was immediately concerned - it was extremely dusty, and far rougher than anticipated, even for a bare granite unit. I like a fair grit in my mortars, more than the rest of my family who primarily use marble. I figured this wouldn't be too bad, and could lead to culinary experimentation.

That is, if I could ever get it clean! Four days and eight washes later and my hand STILL came up sparkly every time I touched it. I decided that it was probably due to the rough-hewn nature of the workmanship, and maybe the seasoning process would solve the problem.

Boy, was I wrong. I ground the first handful of white rice into dust, which seemed to be about 50% stone once I was done, and I noticed some concerning marks developing on the inside of the bowl. I decided to press forward with the seasoning process.

On the very first stroke on the second bowl of rice, the pestle snapped in my hand, cutting my right hand palm in the process. I have never broken one this way before and I have never heard of it happening to anyone else, so I thought it may be a fluke - till I checked the reviews for this item and discovered many other folks in the same position. So no, I will not be replacing it for another granite unit from ChefSofi.Number one. This is NOT a molcajete, but comes up as such in search. My bad for not reading properly. Its a fine mortar and pestle, but definitely not a molcajete. Going to need to buy something else to really fit my needs. That said, ya, I guess it is a mortar and pestle. Please don't include this in search results for "molcajete" as it is 100% not one. It has 20k + reviews, but again, this is my fault for not reading the description. You could totally find a proper molcajete at a local mexican market/bodega. Thank you and hopefully this review helps others who are looking at this item.I just received my mortar and pestle from Amazon today. I opened the package to inspect the items to make sure there was no damage to them from the shipping process. While both the mortar and pestle are not broken, I did however find 2 large mouse/ small rat droppings in the bowl and at least another three within the bubble wrap package on the outside of the bowl.
I find this extremely concerning due to the fact that this is supposed to be a food safe item that is a porous material, and also due to the fact that it can not be properly sanitized. No amount of bleach or boiling water will be able to get into every pore of the mortar to sanitize it. The box was not damaged and did not show any signs of a rodent getting into the amazon or the products packaging. So the contamination would have to had to have happened when it was being made and or packed at the manufacturing site. I don’t know if this is a one off instance or if this is an ongoing problem.
Due to concerns, we will be returning this item.Unfortunately, the pestle arrived broken in both the original mortar and pestle kit and the free replacement shipment. In both cases, the broken pestle was wrapped in a paper (see attached picture) that essentially stated this is a fragile item and that damage can occur during shipping.

From my perspective, the company failed to properly package the item for shipping (twice!). I expected this fragile item to be wrapped with extra bubble wrap to ensure its safe arrival.

Sorry, ChefSofi team, you got a second chance, but you blew it again.I purchased this product in January, I dropped the pestle in April after 3-4 uses; all of maybe 2 feet, from the floor (and that’s being generous) indoors and it split in half. Amazon support provided what could essentially be deemed the worst customer support ever when I attempted to ascertain warranty and or replacement information, rife with incorrect information (actually lies) and no solution. My interaction with the seller to this point hasn’t fared much better.Like. Actually. Maybe it's supposed to be a little rough, for ya know, grinding purposes, but it shouldn't feel like I'm sandpapering away my hands for holding it for less than five minutes. Like, literally. My hands hurt. Minutes later. I've used other mortar and pestles for extended periods with no pain. I didn't even know it was possible to be this awful. I don't have eczema. Heck, I've handled sharpening my knives on a whetstone and sandpapering wood both for extended periods of time. But what did it. A bowl. I'm honestly impressed. So. If you want something low quality to slice up your hands, 10/10 would buy again (/s)I kept grinding and it made whatever I grind gray. For example, I ground garlic and it came out a dark gray because the particles wouldn't stop coming off the mortar/pestle. I seasoned the mortar and pestle as directed but maybe I did something wrong? But I couldn't get it to stop coming out gray.Already own a granite mortar and pestle, got this as a gift for someone. Upon inspection, both the mortar and pestle requires an EXTENSIVE amount of refinement in excess of normal seasoning you'd do for a normal set of granite mortar and pestle to smooth out and eliminate excessive granite dust from being ground. You want grooves in a mortar to catch the food for crushing and grinding, but the grooves cut into the set appears to be very crude with deep and jagged texture in the bowl.

Following the steps in the box will lead to hours of grinding rice that still yields significant amount of granite matter. Do your self a favor and get a better set or if you already purchased one, take a 100 grit sandpaper to the bowl and pestle for about 5 minutes each and then go to a 400 grit for another 5 minutes. Then proceed to the actual seasoning instructions to prepare the set for use, otherwise this will be a good paper weight.Disappointed that America’s Test Kitchen recommended this. It has been such a long journey finding a mortar and pestle but I had to return this.

The interior is truly unpolished and would have been perfect to hold ingredients in place, but it was not true granite. The smell was far fouler and the stone appeared to be grinded pieces of what looked like granite put together with binders of some sort. The pattern is nothing like granite. It was far too uniform and lacked the characteristics of true granite. I question if it’s even safe to use this for food. I got another mortar and pestle on Amazon that explicitly stated 100% solid granite and It is worlds apart from this one.
